title: ls/ready exit 0 when format-version sentinel points at blob instead of commit
body:
# Repro

Script: `experiments/qa-redteam-2026-06-25/sub3-panic.sh` function `c1`

```bash
# Scratch repo with one issue created normally, then:
blob="$(echo "not a commit" | git hash-object -w --stdin)"
git update-ref refs/jjf/meta/format-version "$blob"
jjf ls     # exits 0 — should exit 1 with typed error
jjf ready  # exits 0 — should exit 1 with typed error
```

# Observed

`jjf ls` and `jjf ready` exit 0 and return normal output despite the format-version sentinel ref pointing at a blob OID rather than a commit OID. The `detect_storage_mode` function in `crates/jjf-storage/src/lib.rs:1047` calls `resolve_ref` and returns `StorageMode::V3` for any `Ok(Some(_))` — including blobs — because `resolve_ref` only checks presence, not object type.

The docstring at `lib.rs:1037` states "Returns `StorageMode::V3` iff the sentinel ref resolves to a **commit**" but the implementation does not validate the object type.

`jjf show roadmap` correctly exits 2 (slug not found, expected).

# Expected

`jjf ls` and `jjf ready` should detect the corrupt sentinel (blob, not commit) and exit 1 with a typed error envelope `{"ok":false,"error":{"kind":"...","message":"format-version sentinel is not a commit"}}`. Per `crates/jjf-storage/src/lib.rs:1037`: "Returns V3 iff the sentinel ref resolves to a commit."

# Severity rationale

No `thread 'main' panicked` in this run — jjf silently accepted corrupt repo state and returned exit 0 with apparently valid output, masking the corruption; the sev:panic label is assigned per QA sub-pass 3 triage rules for C1-class sentinel-corruption attacks.
